author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: DeepSource zero-issues program: triage and clear the 2026-07-14 baseline (~11k reported occurrences)

Every DeepSource category has a child item; the ruleset-alignment child lands FIRST so remaining counts reflect real signal; each child clears its category to 0 via fixes or documented per-rule .deepsource.toml suppressions (every suppression carries a rationale); dashboard re-checked after each child and counts noted here; program closes at 0 open issues on app.deepsource.com/gh/unbraind/pm-cli/issues

message: Maintainer directive 2026-07-14: fix all DeepSource-reported issues. Baseline from the public dashboard (analysis active since activation, commit d062b573): All ~11k — Bug Risk 1.2k, Anti-pattern 7.2k, Documentation 2.8k, Performance 36, Style 16, Typecheck 2, Security 0, Secrets 0, Coverage 0; 'Recommended' view 586. Commit statuses are green because only NEW/blocking issues gate — the 11k are legacy stock. EXECUTION NOTE: the per-rule breakdown is login-gated for anonymous sessions; enumerate via the maintainer's dashboard session or a DeepSource PAT (Settings→API access) before starting any child; record the top rules by occurrence as a note here. Bulk counts are dominated by DeepSource's default JS ruleset overlapping/conflicting with this repo's stricter ESLint+jscpd+docstring gates — config alignment, not mass code edits, is the correct first move.

author: codex-audit-20260715

patch.value: Align DeepSource with repository policy, then drive every remaining analyzer category to zero through real fixes or narrowly justified rule suppressions.
